findings.title: CWE-22: User-controlled path reaches file API in `expandPlaylist()`
findings.description: File API uses potentially user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Validate paths against an allowlist.

findings.title: CWE-78: OS command injection in `launchUpdatedInstance()`
findings.description: Runtime.exec/ProcessBuilder with user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Avoid passing user input to exec/ProcessBuilder.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak random number generator in `deriveTempFile()`
findings.description: java.util.Random or Math.random() used — not cryptographically secure.
findings.suggestion: Use java.security.SecureRandom for security-sensitive randomness.

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak cryptographic algorithm (MD5) at line 297
findings.description: `getInstance("MD5")` at L297. MD5 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

findings.title: CWE-327: Weak cryptographic hash in `calculateMD5()`
findings.description: MD5 or SHA-1 used for hashing — vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 via MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256").

findings.title: Interprocedural SQLi taint: getNodeJsExecutablen() at line 260
findings.description: Tainted argument flows to SQL sink through `getNodeJsExecutablen()`. Call at L260: `getNodeJsExecutablen(installationPath, properties)`.
findings.suggestion: Review the data flow through helper methods. Ensure parameterized queries.

findings.title: CWE-78: OS command injection in `parseMarkdown()`
findings.description: Runtime.exec/ProcessBuilder with user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Avoid passing user input to exec/ProcessBuilder.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS via unencoded response write in `generate()`
findings.description: response.getWriter().write() outputs user-controlled data without HTML encoding.
findings.suggestion: Encode output with an HTML encoder or use a template engine that auto-escapes.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak random number generator in `createTableModelModifier()`
findings.description: java.util.Random or Math.random() used — not cryptographically secure.
findings.suggestion: Use java.security.SecureRandom for security-sensitive randomness.

findings.title: CWE-78: OS command injection in `oggFileToMp3()`
findings.description: Runtime.exec/ProcessBuilder with user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Avoid passing user input to exec/ProcessBuilder.

findings.title: CWE-1188: Debug mode enabled in Java at line 8
findings.description: Debug mode is enabled — this may leak stack traces, internal state, or sensitive data in production.
findings.suggestion: Gate debug mode behind an environment variable or Spring profile: `@Profile("dev")`.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak random number generator in `initData()`
findings.description: java.util.Random or Math.random() used — not cryptographically secure.
findings.suggestion: Use java.security.SecureRandom for security-sensitive randomness.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded credential at line 61
findings.description: Hardcoded secret value found at L61: `123...`.
findings.suggestion: Use environment variables, a secrets manager, or a configuration server.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded credential in Java source
findings.description: A password/apiKey/secret literal is assigned directly in code.
findings.suggestion: Move credentials to environment variables or a secrets manager and rotate the exposed value.

findings.title: CWE-78: OS command injection in `testProcessBuilder()`
findings.description: Runtime.exec/ProcessBuilder with user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Avoid passing user input to exec/ProcessBuilder.

findings.title: CWE-78: OS command injection in `testFFmpeg()`
findings.description: Runtime.exec/ProcessBuilder with user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Avoid passing user input to exec/ProcessBuilder.

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak hash via `DigestUtils.md5Hex()` at line 235
findings.description: `DigestUtils.md5Hex()` at L235. MD5 is cryptographically broken.
findings.suggestion: Use `DigestUtils.sha256Hex()` or `DigestUtils.sha512Hex()` instead.

findings.title: CWE-22: User-controlled path reaches file API in `uploadChunk()`
findings.description: File API uses potentially user-controlled input.
findings.suggestion: Validate paths against an allowlist.

findings.title: CWE-327: Weak cryptographic hash in `checkFileMd5()`
findings.description: MD5 or SHA-1 used for hashing — vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 via MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256").

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak cryptographic algorithm (MD5) at line 126
findings.description: `getInstance("MD5")` at L126. MD5 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

findings.title: CWE-327: Weak cryptographic hash in `getStringMD5()`
findings.description: MD5 or SHA-1 used for hashing — vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 via MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256").
